content: Configurable ICM curiosity mechanisms for 2025 swarm UAV autonomous agents can be adapted to dynamically allocate an agent’s limited working memory capacity by prioritizing memory storage for only the novel, curiosity-triggered states the agent encounters, rather than caching all observed information. This creates a self-regulating memory system where intrinsic reward for novelty acts as a natural filter for working memory bottlenecks.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
